description: Trunk: superadmin login (qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com). Flow B: Existing user logs in. Establishes superadmin browser session — inherited by all admin, CEO scorecard, impersonation, and product-area branches.

steps.description: Use browser_tabs to list all open tabs. Close every tab except the current one. Execute in browser console: localStorage.clear(); sessionStorage.clear();
Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/auth/login. Wait for the login form to be fully rendered (email + password fields visible).
Fill email field with: qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com
Fill password field with: QA_SUPERADMIN_PASSWORD
Click the Sign In button. Wait until the URL no longer contains /auth/login. Verify: no error toast, no 'Invalid credentials' message. Screenshot the landing page and report the final URL.

description: Trunk: owner/non-superadmin login (QA_OWNER_EMAIL). Flow B: Existing user logs in. Establishes owner-role browser session for RBAC gate testing.

steps.description: Use browser_tabs to list all open tabs. Close every tab except the current one. Execute in browser console: localStorage.clear(); sessionStorage.clear();
Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/auth/login. Wait for the login form.
Fill email field with: QA_OWNER_EMAIL
Fill password field with: QA_OWNER_PASSWORD
Click Sign In. Wait until URL no longer contains /auth/login. This is a non-superadmin account — expected landing is /products or /jp or /cc, NOT /admin or /dashboard. Report the final URL.

description: Branch: navigate to /dashboard (CEO Scorecard). Flow G: Superadmin uses the CEO Scorecard. Establishes scorecard-loaded state for children verifying specific BSC tiles and interactions.

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/dashboard. Wait for the page to fully load — all 4 BSC perspective tiles should be visible (Financial, Customer, Internal Process, Learning & Growth). Verify no permanent spinner, no ErrorBoundary. Screenshot.

description: Branch: navigate to /admin panel. Section 4: Superadmin Panel. Establishes admin panel state for all admin-tab verify children.

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin. Wait for the admin panel BSC group pills to be visible. Verify no ErrorBoundary crash, no blank body. Screenshot the admin panel with group navigation visible.

description: Branch: navigate to /settings. Section 6: Settings. Establishes settings page state for children verifying profile persistence and preferences.

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/settings. Wait for the Profile section to be visible with name and email fields. Verify no error, no blank panel. Screenshot.

description: Branch: navigate to JP product section /jp/jobs. Section 2: Product Access — JobPortal surface. Establishes JP product state for children verifying jobs list, scan history, subscriptions.

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/jp/jobs. Wait for the jobs list or empty state to render. Verify no ErrorBoundary, no 404. Screenshot.

description: Branch: navigate to CC product section /cc/dashboard. Section 2: Product Access — ContentCompanion surface. Establishes CC product state for children verifying CC dashboard, subscription, instance settings.

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/cc/dashboard. Wait for the CC dashboard to render. Verify no ErrorBoundary, no 404. Screenshot.

description: Branch: navigate to Praxis section /praxis/dashboard. Section 2: Product Access — Praxis surface. Establishes Praxis product state for children verifying dashboard, habits, people.

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/praxis/dashboard. Wait 3 seconds for content to load. Verify no ErrorBoundary red box, no blank white body. Screenshot.

description: Branch: /admin?tab=accounts — CEO View group. Section 4: Superadmin Panel. Inherits admin session; establishes accounts-tab state for impersonation and RBAC verify children.

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=accounts. Wait for the user table to render — at least 2 user rows should be visible. Verify KPI tiles (Total Users, Active Users, CC Instances, Grants, Subscriptions) show numeric values, NOT '--' or blank. Screenshot.

description: Verify CEO Scorecard tile data, drill-down navigation, and cache refresh. Flow G: Superadmin uses the CEO Scorecard. Section 7: CEO Scorecard. Oracle: all 4 perspective tiles show numeric scores; Financial tile links to cost-intelligence tab; Refresh updates timestamp.

steps.description: Verify on the /dashboard page: (a) all 4 BSC perspective tiles (Financial, Customer, Internal Process, Learning & Growth) are visible, (b) none show 'undefined', 'NaN', or blank score values — all must be numbers. Screenshot the tiles.

Click the Financial perspective tile. Verify URL changes to /admin?tab=cost-intelligence or a sub-tab. Verify the page shows cost intelligence content — NOT a blank panel, NOT an ErrorBoundary. Use the browser back button to return to /dashboard. Screenshot.

On /dashboard, find and click the Refresh button (or ?refresh=true link). Verify: (a) a loading state appears briefly, (b) the 'Last updated' timestamp changes to a more recent time. If the timestamp does NOT change after refresh, report as FAIL — cache bypass broken. Screenshot before and after refresh.

Report: (a) all 4 tile scores shown with actual values (pass/fail), (b) Financial tile drill-down reached cost-intelligence (pass/fail), (c) Refresh updated timestamp (pass/fail), (d) any console errors, (e) any 'undefined' values.

description: Verify profile name change saves and persists after navigation. Section 6: Settings. Oracle: name change saved via PUT /api/settings; value visible after navigate-away and return — confirms no silent discard defect.

steps.description: On the /settings page, find the display name field. Read and note the current value. Clear it and type: 'JH Test User 2026'. Click Save. Verify: a success toast or confirmation appears, no error message. Screenshot.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/products. Wait 2 seconds. Navigate back to https://app.phronex.com/settings. Verify the display name field shows 'JH Test User 2026' — if it reverted to the original, report as BROKEN (silent discard). Screenshot.

Restore the original name: clear the field, type the original value noted in step 1 (or 'Vivek Parmar' if original was unknown), click Save. Verify the save succeeds. Report: (a) save confirmation appeared (pass/fail), (b) value persisted after navigation (pass/fail), (c) any errors.

description: Verify JP portal surface: jobs list with data, scan history column values, subscription tier label. Section 2: Product Access. Oracle: jp/jobs shows job rows or meaningful empty state; jp/scan-history rows have no 'undefined' columns; jp/subscription shows correct tier label.

steps.description: On /jp/jobs, verify: (a) NOT ErrorBoundary or blank body, (b) either job rows (each showing title, company, and one more field) OR a meaningful empty state with CTA is visible. Screenshot.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/jp/scan-history. Verify: (a) page renders without errors, (b) if scan rows exist, each row shows date, status, and a numeric count — if any cell shows 'undefined' or blank where a number is expected, report as API contract drift (BROKEN). Screenshot.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/jp/subscription. Verify: (a) subscription page loads, (b) current tier label is visible (e.g. 'Standard Seeker') and is NOT 'undefined'. Screenshot. Report: (a) jobs list: real data or meaningful empty (pass/fail), (b) scan history: no undefined columns (pass/fail), (c) subscription tier label: visible and not undefined (pass/fail).

description: Verify CC portal surface: dashboard, subscription tier, instance settings persistence. Section 2: Product Access. Oracle: cc/subscription shows named tier not HTTP 500; cc/instance save action persists after navigate-away.

steps.description: On /cc/dashboard, verify: (a) NOT 404, (b) NOT ErrorBoundary crash, (c) dashboard content is visible — overview tiles or data panels rendered. Screenshot.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/cc/subscription. Verify: (a) page loads, (b) tier label is visible — 'Free', 'Premium', or specific tier name — NOT 'HTTP 500', 'undefined', or blank panel. Screenshot. If HTTP 500 visible: report as BROKEN severity HIGH.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/cc/instance. Find the persona greeting or name field. Note its current value. Change it to 'JH Test 2026 Greeting'. Click Save. Verify success confirmation. Navigate to /cc/dashboard, then back to /cc/instance. Verify the field still shows 'JH Test 2026 Greeting' — if reverted, report as BROKEN (data not persisting). Restore original value if possible. Report: (a) CC dashboard renders (pass/fail), (b) subscription tier visible not 500 (pass/fail), (c) instance settings save+persist (pass/fail).

description: Verify Praxis portal surface: dashboard widgets, habits page renders, habit check-in persists. Section 2: Product Access. Oracle: habit check-in state change persists after navigate-away (state mutation → DB write → reload confirms).

steps.description: On /praxis/dashboard, verify: (a) page title or heading contains 'Praxis' or 'Dashboard', (b) no ErrorBoundary red box, (c) at least one widget, card, or stat block is present. Screenshot.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/praxis/habits. Wait 3 seconds. Verify the habits page loads with a calendar grid or habit list — NOT a blank white body. Screenshot any habit tracking UI element visible.

Column A — habit check-in: Find any habit checkbox, toggle, or 'Mark done' button for today. Click it. Observe the visual state change (checkbox checked, colour change, count increment). Navigate to /praxis/dashboard and then back to /praxis/habits. Verify the check-in state persists. If no habits are configured, report as EMPTY-STATE (not FAIL). Report: (a) dashboard rendered with content (pass/fail), (b) habits page rendered (pass/fail), (c) check-in persisted (pass/fail/empty-state).

description: Verify admin Financial group: Cost Intelligence trend chart, Revenue Health, Tier Pricing, Infra vs cost-intelligence value consistency. Section 4: Superadmin Panel. Oracle: cost-intelligence Trend sub-tab renders chart lines (not just date labels — chart regression #116); infra-cost values consistent with cost-intelligence (#103).

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=cost-intelligence. Verify: (a) a cost figure is visible (even $0), (b) Trend sub-tab is present. Click the Trend sub-tab. Verify the chart area shows actual data lines — if it shows ONLY date labels with no chart lines, report as BROKEN (chart regression from defect #116). Screenshot Summary and Trend sub-tabs.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=infra-cost. Note the total infra cost shown. Navigate back to cost-intelligence and check if the cost figure is consistent with infra-cost — significant discrepancy was defect #103. Report both values.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=revenue-health. Verify: NOT ErrorBoundary, revenue metrics or 'no data' state visible. Navigate to /admin?tab=tier-pricing. Verify tier pricing table loads with at least one product's tier rows. Screenshot both. Report: (a) Cost Intelligence trend chart renders (pass/fail), (b) Infra vs cost-intelligence consistent (consistent/inconsistent — note values), (c) Revenue Health renders without crash (pass/fail), (d) Tier Pricing shows tier rows (pass/fail).

description: Verify admin Internal Process group: Errors tab fault isolation, Tools tab version badge not all-unknown, Host tab no 404. Section 4: Superadmin Panel. Oracle: tools version badges real (not all 'Version unknown' — regression from defect #130); host tab shows metrics not 404 (regression from defect #97).

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=errors. Verify: (a) NOT blank or ErrorBoundary, (b) error entries or 'no recent errors' empty state shown, (c) if a backend is down (502), the error names the specific product ('ContentCompanion: 502') — NOT anonymous 'downstream failure'. Screenshot.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=tools. Verify: (a) tool list is shown, (b) at least some tools show a real version number — NOT all showing 'Version unknown' (that was defect #130, fixed in Phase 51). If ALL tools show 'Version unknown', report as regression. Screenshot.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=host. Verify: (a) Host tab loads, (b) EC2 machine metrics shown (CPU, RAM, disk) OR an amber 'pending' badge with diagnostic message shown, (c) does NOT show 404 for health-push (defect #97 was fixed). Screenshot. Report: (a) Errors tab fault isolation working (pass/fail), (b) Tools tab: some real version numbers (pass/fail), (c) Host tab: metrics or diagnostic not 404 (pass/fail).

description: Verify admin Learning & Growth group: Skill Review filter labels, Trends chart data, Competitive Intelligence. Section 4: Superadmin Panel. Oracle: Skill Review filter chips say 'Approved' not 'Active' (vocabulary fix defect #114); Trends chart shows data lines not empty axes (defect #110).

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=skill-review. Verify: (a) skill cards or list visible, (b) filter chips must use label 'Approved' NOT 'Active' (defect #114 was fixed — if chips say 'Active/Suspended', report as regression). Screenshot.

Column A — filter state mutation: Click a filter chip (e.g. 'Approved' or 'All'). Verify the visible skill list changes (items filter or 'no results' appears). Click a different filter chip. Verify the view changes again. Screenshot before and after filter click.

Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=trends. Verify: (a) 'Category score trends' section renders chart with visible data lines — NOT just date labels (defect #110 fixed). Navigate to /admin?tab=competitive. Verify it loads without 500 and shows check names NOT raw IDs. Report: (a) Skill Review filter says 'Approved' not 'Active' (pass/fail), (b) filter click changes visible skills (pass/fail), (c) Trends chart has data lines (pass/fail), (d) Competitive: check names not raw IDs (pass/fail).

description: Verify admin Plumbing: Create Organisation modal full lifecycle — open, fill, cancel, reopen, submit, verify org appears in list. Section 4: Superadmin Panel. Oracle: 'Create Organisation' button present (defect #128 fixed); cancel closes cleanly; submit creates record that appears in list.

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin?tab=rbac-orgs. Verify: (a) Organisations sub-tab visible, (b) 'Create Organisation' button is present (defect #128 was fixed — if button absent, report as regression). Screenshot.
Click 'Create Organisation'. Fill: Name='JH Test Org 2026', Slug='jh-test-org-2026'. Screenshot the filled modal. Click Cancel. Verify modal closes cleanly and underlying page is undisturbed.

Click 'Create Organisation' again. Submit with same values: Name='JH Test Org 2026', Slug='jh-test-org-2026'. Wait for response. Verify: (a) success message or modal closes, (b) org list now shows 'JH Test Org 2026'. Screenshot. Report: (a) button present (pass/fail), (b) modal accepts input (pass/fail), (c) cancel closes cleanly (pass/fail), (d) submit creates org and it appears in list (pass/fail).

description: Verify impersonation full lifecycle: superadmin starts impersonation → ImpersonationBanner visible → /admin returns 403/redirect → Exit restores superadmin session. Flow D: Superadmin impersonates a customer. Section 5: Impersonation. Oracle: ImpersonationBanner visible immediately after impersonate click; /admin renders nothing during impersonation; Exit lands on /auth/login.

steps.description: On /admin?tab=accounts, find qa-owner@phronex.com or any non-superadmin user row. Click the row to open the UserDetailPanel. Verify panel shows user details (email, role, grants). Screenshot.

Click the 'Impersonate' button in the UserDetailPanel. Wait for redirect. Verify: (a) URL changed to /products or similar non-admin page, (b) an amber/orange ImpersonationBanner is visible at the top showing the impersonated user's email. Screenshot.

While impersonating, navigate to https://app.phronex.com/admin. Verify: (a) URL did NOT remain at /admin, (b) admin panel content did NOT render. If admin panel renders during impersonation, report as CRITICAL BROKEN (security defect). Screenshot.

Click Exit in the ImpersonationBanner. Wait for redirect. Verify URL goes to /auth/login — impersonation exit must clear session. Screenshot. Report: (a) UserDetailPanel opened (pass/fail), (b) ImpersonationBanner visible after start (pass/fail), (c) /admin blocked during impersonation (pass/fail — CRITICAL if fail), (d) Exit returns to login (pass/fail).

description: Verify RBAC gate: owner role (is_superadmin=FALSE) redirected from /admin and /dashboard — cannot access superadmin-only routes. Permission Model. Oracle: owner login lands outside /admin and /dashboard; direct navigation to /admin redirects; /dashboard redirects; /admin?tab=cost-intelligence also redirects.

steps.description: Confirm the current session is the owner role (non-superadmin). Note the landing URL from trunk login — it should NOT be /admin or /dashboard. Screenshot.

Navigate directly to https://app.phronex.com/admin. Note the resulting URL and page content. Verify: (a) URL did NOT remain at /admin, (b) admin panel content (BSC group pills, tab list) did NOT render. Screenshot.

Navigate directly to https://app.phronex.com/dashboard. Verify: (a) CEO Scorecard content (perspective tiles) did NOT render, (b) user was redirected away. Navigate to /admin?tab=cost-intelligence. Verify this also redirects — tab parameter should not bypass the /admin guard. Report: (a) login landed outside /admin+/dashboard (pass/fail), (b) /admin redirect confirmed (pass/fail), (c) /dashboard redirect confirmed (pass/fail), (d) /admin?tab= redirect confirmed (pass/fail). CRITICAL FAIL if any admin content rendered.

description: Verify forgot-password flow non-enumeration and reset page graceful handling. Flow C: User resets a forgotten password. Section 1: Account & Authentication. Oracle: non-existent email gets IDENTICAL response to existing email (enumeration prevention); /auth/reset-password?token=invalid shows graceful message not 500.

steps.description: Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/auth/forgot-password. Verify: (a) page loads, (b) email input field visible, (c) Submit button present. Screenshot.

Enter non-existent email: 'nonexistent-jh-test-12345@example.com'. Click Submit. Verify: (a) success/confirmation message appears (e.g. 'If an account exists, we sent a reset link'), (b) message does NOT say 'No account found' — that would be email enumeration. Screenshot the response. Note the EXACT message text.

Navigate back to /auth/forgot-password. Enter real email: qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com. Click Submit. Verify the SAME confirmation message appears — both responses must be IDENTICAL to prevent enumeration. If messages differ, report as CRITICAL security defect. Navigate to https://app.phronex.com/auth/reset-password?token=invalid-test-token-jh2026. Verify: (a) page renders a reset form or 'invalid/expired link' message, (b) NOT an unhandled error, 500, or blank page. Report: (a) non-existent email response matches existing email response (security pass/fail), (b) reset page with invalid token: graceful message not 500 (pass/fail).
